threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You queue at the coffee shop without knowing to order at the counter first. The locals know that's a rookie mistake.
Last night on the 59, a bloke was arguing with the driver about where to get off—turns out he missed his stop three times and was just too proud to admit it.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I realized I was part of Neukölln? When I saw a guy in a suit play chess with a street cat outside the Kiezkneipe.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg can you find a kebab shop doubling as a vinyl record store. Grab a döner while hunting for your next favorite album—it’s a dream combo.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can tell a local by their disdain for BART’s 'sudden' delays; we expect the chaos like fog rolling in. Tourists still seem shocked.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy juggling on the 21 bus yesterday while a lady was trying to sell incense. Only in SF do you get a live show with your commute.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is all about Gaudí, but the real charm's in the tiny, dusty bars where locals still drink vermut on a weekday afternoon.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell by the way they still ask for directions to the nearest Sagrada Família—like it’s a quick stroll.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still expect the Tube to run on time. Locals just check their phones and shrug when the line's down.
Just saw a guy in a suit on the 333 talking about how he missed the last train. All while two people in the back were totally asleep with pizza in hand.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I saw a guy in a full tuxedo casually buying döner at 3 AM—now that's Neukölln for you.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin can you catch a punk rock concert in an abandoned subway station, right next to a lavish cake shop. The contrasts are wild here.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can spot a local by their calm indifference to the BART delays—while newcomers are busy complaining about it on their phones.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy serenading the #19 bus passengers with a ukulele. Not sure if it helped the delays, but it definitely lightened the mood.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think all locals eat paella every day. Most of us prefer a simple plat de casa, especially on a chill evening.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell they're new by the way they order a café con leche without knowing it’s just 'un café'. Still holding onto their home cup size.
